title: Have you felt obsessed with Eva Are you obsessed with Eva
display_title: Have you felt obsessed with Eva Are you obsessed with Eva
wikitext:
[]: Have you felt obsessed with Eva?/Are you obsessed with Eva?             Quote Post by bap_120790 » Sun Nov 11, 2007 8:54 am I'm not sure if I am, but it's been occupying my thoughts for sometime now.

[]:             Quote Post by Thamaker » Sun Nov 11, 2007 9:23 am don't worry. you are not alone..

[]: Re: Have you felt obsessed with Eva?/Are you obsessed with E             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 11, 2007 9:34 am After I'd watched the series and the EoE, it took me months to let it go.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Nov 11, 2007 10:20 am I think obsession is an ugly word. I think it's more applicable to an unhealthy domination of one's thoughts by one thing and that thing only. I think even the most hard core of fans are not necessarily obsessed, but perhaps just actively and consistently interested. Most people have passions. And I don't think most would think it an obsession if a person had a deep passion for music, for example. Even if an anime series is an odd thing to be passionate for, I don't think it's so odd to be considered an unhealthy obsession. But yes, by my own definition, I am actively and consistently interested in NGE. It's a hard experience to shake, especially when it resonates strongly with you personally. I'm writing an article on that very thing right now.

[]:             Quote Post by birdyofthemoongoddess » Sun Nov 11, 2007 12:07 pm Definitely not.

[]:             Quote Post by Squiggy » Sun Nov 11, 2007 12:09 pm I consider myself more of a casual Eva Fan rather than a hard core one.

[]:             Quote Post by Squiggy » Sun Nov 11, 2007 12:10 pm Thamaker wrote: don't worry. you are (not) alone..

[]: Re: Have you felt obsessed with Eva?/Are you obsessed with E             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Nov 11, 2007 12:17 pm Mr. Tines wrote: After I'd watched the series and the EoE, it took me months to let it go.

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Sun Nov 11, 2007 12:18 pm "Actively interested" is a good way to put it. I'm not obsessed, and my interest comes and goes in waves, but it consistently returns because there's just so much to think about when it comes to Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Sun Nov 11, 2007 1:44 pm but, Birdy, you ARE obsessed with Evageeks.org/forum... Nah, I've been really into it these past few months, but that's just cause I just discovered it. If it doesn't calm down by the time the commentary is done, then maybe I'm a little obsessed.

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Sun Nov 11, 2007 6:56 pm For a few months I was...then it was calmed down...then it disappered and now returned to a level where its just one of my passions.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Nov 11, 2007 8:32 pm ^same here^

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sun Nov 11, 2007 9:25 pm No, because Rahxephon is better , MWAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA!!!!!!!! * runs away from angry lynch mob * Slim: Frank... that is so low... hell you don't even l ike anime, period . Frank: There are FAR more important things to be obessed over, FOOl! Such as the aquistion of material and man power, the step-by-step articulating of the taking over a nation, the captivation of the enormous lay-*is cuts off* Slim: -ips of Angelina Jolie? Frank:... FOOL! DO NOT EVER MOCK THE NAME OF MY FUTURE QUEEN! Slim: Really, I don't get your 'lip' obsession ... then again you lack hormones as a disembodied voice so it's surprising you feel "attracted" to anything at all. Frank: FOOL! Once, I had access to your hormones, before the ' Luke ' incident, and at the time I assessed Ms. Joalie to be the most worthy of me... Slim: Right, thanks to SPIKE TV and Botox , although she was only #2 on their list.... Frank: And I shall rectify that error through the elimination of Beyoncé Knowles!!! Slim: I say take them both down, neither are that attra- ...ah crap * runs away from yet another angry lynch mob * Anyway, is Evangelion an obsession for me? Yes and no, I'm a bit analytical over what it teaches, like to make in-depth studies of the characters, and I now relate many classic pop songs to it, but I'm not crazy over the show itself, meaning I'm not desperate to get my hands on or read of everything marked 'Eva', as the only merchandise you'll ever find of it on me, or any anime for that matter, is the DVD/VHS recordings of the episodes/films (excluding the few issues of the Trigun manga). I'm not into fanfiction anymore due to reasons I've explained in another thread, I can't draw that well, nor do I try to, and I rarely talk about anime in the 'real world', and even then it's usually not Evangelion , but something more mainstream. (The one time I talked about Evangelion with a family member, they convinced me for a short time that I was in-fact pronouncing the title wrong, with the ‘g’ supposed to be making a ’j’ sound, like you would when pronouncing the word “Evangelist“. Turns out, that is actually a perversion of the English language at large, had it followed the proper provisions, “Evangelist” would sound as “Evangelion” does.)

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Nov 11, 2007 9:29 pm I also am not interested in supplemental material and merchandising. I basically consider my only "obsessions" (or "major passion", whatever you refer) great film and music. NGE just falls into one of those categories and happens to be one of its greatest achievements imHo.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Sun Nov 11, 2007 9:53 pm I've never let anything in my life get to the point where I start relating life to an anime and then there was EVA

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Nov 11, 2007 10:23 pm I first watched this show around spring of 2002. I was pretty obsessed with it for almost a year. Fortunately, that period of time is over. Now my only business is annoying the hell out of everyone on this forum. And business is good. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Nov 11, 2007 10:26 pm After all, its what the true Brikoriri would do!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 11, 2007 10:30 pm When my medication isn't working or I pour unhealthy hours into NGE-related crap when I'm patently supposed to be doing other things (like right now), I'm obsessed. Otherwise, kind of what Jimbo said.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Nov 11, 2007 10:31 pm if thats the case, good to have you back.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Nov 11, 2007 10:36 pm Reichu took the words out of my mouth except for the meds part. Besides that, I agree with Yojimbo.
